mnemonics: Z-SCORE: Z-test for Samples, Calculations, Observations, Reliability, Estimation. A mnemonic for recalling the key components and purpose of the test.
keyPoints: A large sample implies n > 30, allowing use of the Central Limit Theorem for a normal sampling distribution.
The test statistic is a Z-score: Z = (Sample_Statistic - Population_Parameter) / Standard_Error.
The standard error for a proportion is √(p(1-p)/n); for a mean, it is σ/√n (use sample standard deviation 's' if σ is unknown).
commonMistakes: Using a t-test instead of a z-test for large samples (n>30). The z-test is simpler and appropriate here.
Incorrectly calculating the standard error, e.g., using the formula for a proportion when testing a mean.
Misinterpreting the p-value: a low p-value (≤ α) means you reject the null hypothesis, not that you accept the alternative hypothesis as absolutely true.
